cases.prompt_body: Architectural documentary photograph inside the Hyatt Regency Crown Center atrium, fourth-floor suspended walkway underside and box-beam hanger-rod connection visible as part of real overhead walkway geometry, tall glass wall, brass balcony rails, warm concrete, available atrium light, credible lens perspective from a balcony or stair landing, doubled-load stress implied only by subtle tension in paired rods and compressed connection shadows, no isolated hardware, no white void, no colored object, no sculpture, no diagram, no collapse

Photographic documentary view of the Hyatt Regency Crown Center atrium before the failure, low-to-mid view from the lobby floor with suspended walkways crossing overhead, tall glass walls, balcony rail bands, open floor, sunken lounge edge, warm concrete, brass, glass, plants, available hotel light, a few small anonymous event figures secondary in scale, wrongness carried only by a heavy overhead walkway shadow and compressed architectural weight, no colored prop, no red object, no spectacle

Quiet empty architectural documentary photograph inside the Hyatt Regency Crown Center atrium after the crowd has cleared, open floor plane, sunken lounge edge, tall glass wall, balcony rail bands, suspended walkway shadows, real carpet, tile, concrete, brass, glass, and plant textures under available atrium light, absence carried by scale, shadow, and stillness only, no people, no colored prop, no red object, no debris field, no emergency scene
